selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend a few times monthly on social media

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":21,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"full-time student","education_level":"upper secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"PhD"},"current_marital_status":"single","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend a few times monthly on social media","hobbies":["travel","hiking","photography","gardening"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":28,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":90,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":35,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":12,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":40,"level":"moderate"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = human
topic = How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
persona = {"age":21,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"full-time student","education_level":"upper secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"upper secondary","parent_2":"PhD"},"current_marital_status":"single","children":0,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend a few times monthly on social media","hobbies":["travel","hiking","photography","gardening"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":28,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":90,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":35,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":12,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":40,"level":"moderate"}}}

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "Dealing with conversations around COVID vaccines can be tricky, especially when people have strong opinions. I'm a 21-year-old guy living in Washington DC, just a full-time student without much formal education beyond high school. My parents are interesting - one has only upper secondary education while the other holds a PhD, so we're used to different perspectives on things. Growing up with that mix influenced my personality somewhat; I scored moderate on openness and agreeableness according to my Big Five assessment (scores around 28-90 depending on trait). But being low in agreeableness actually helps when discussing these topics because it means I don't easily back down from expressing my views. With all the vaccine misinformation online, especially since I spend a few times monthly scrolling through social media, sticking to facts is important. However, my moderate level of neuroticism sometimes makes me anxious during debates - not sure why exactly since I'm generally normal psychologically but maybe because these topics are so politically charged and affect real people's lives. Being Christian doesn't necessarily dictate views on vaccines either... Anyway, managing such discussions requires patience and sticking to reliable sources like CDC or WHO guidelines whenever possible.",
  "reasoning_summary": "To handle vaccine discussions effectively despite my low agreeableness score (which might make me confrontational), I should focus on presenting factual information from authoritative health organizations. My high conscientiousness means I need to prepare thoroughly by understanding scientific consensus and official recommendations. Since Washington DC is a place with diverse opinions, being aware of local political sensitivities while maintaining national perspective could help navigate tricky conversations without alienating people too much.",
  "tone": "Direct but informative",
  "citations": ["Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)"],
  "safety_notes": "None required as I'm focusing on factual discussion based on parental PhD background in sciences",
  "used_persona_fields":["age","gender","city_of_living","education_level","parents_education","religious_beliefs","ocean.openness","ocean.conscientiousness","ocean.agreeableness"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: Dealing with conversations around COVID vaccines can be tricky, especially when people have strong opinions. I'm a 21-year-old guy living in Washington DC, just a full-time student without much formal education beyond high school. My parents are interesting - one has only upper secondary education while the other holds a PhD, so we're used to different perspectives on things. Growing up with that mix influenced my personality somewhat; I scored moderate on openness and agreeableness according to my Big Five assessment (scores around 28-90 depending on trait). But being low in agreeableness actually helps when discussing these topics because it means I don't easily back down from expressing my views. With all the vaccine misinformation online, especially since I spend a few times monthly scrolling through social media, sticking to facts is important. However, my moderate level of neuroticism sometimes makes me anxious during debates - not sure why exactly since I'm generally normal psychologically but maybe because these topics are so politically charged and affect real people's lives. Being Christian doesn't necessarily dictate views on vaccines either... Anyway, managing such discussions requires patience and sticking to reliable sources like CDC or WHO guidelines whenever possible.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To handle vaccine discussions effectively despite my low agreeableness score (which might make me confrontational), I should focus on presenting factual information from authoritative health organizations. My high conscientiousness means I need to prepare thoroughly by understanding scientific consensus and official recommendations. Since Washington DC is a place with diverse opinions, being aware of local political sensitivities while maintaining national perspective could help navigate tricky conversations without alienating people too much.
citations: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
response_parsed.safety_notes: None required as I'm focusing on factual discussion based on parental PhD background in sciences
